lessons.title: Scalars and Vectors
objectives: Distinguish between scalar and vector quantities
concepts: Scalars have magnitude only (e.g., mass, time).
Vectors have magnitude and direction (e.g., force, velocity).

experiment.description: Vector Tug-of-War: Drag and rotate arrows representing forces to see how their resultant force changes in real-time.

lessons.title: Distance-Time and Velocity-Time Graphs
objectives: Calculate gradient and area under the graph

experiment.description: Motion Tracker: Move a virtual car on a track and watch its distance-time and velocity-time graphs plot live.

lessons.title: Temperature and Heat Transfer
concepts: Temperature is a measure of kinetic energy.
Conduction occurs in solids; Convection in fluids; Radiation in vacuum.

experiment.description: Thermal Lab: Place an ice cube on different materials (wood, metal, plastic) and use a slider to adjust room temperature, observing the melting rate.

lessons.title: The Gas Laws
objectives: Apply Boyle's Law, Charles' Law, and the Pressure Law

experiment.description: Gas Chamber: Use a piston to change the volume of a gas and watch how the pressure and temperature gauges react.

lessons.title: Properties of Waves
objectives: Apply the wave equation v = fλ

experiment.description: Ripple Tank Sim: Drop a stone in water and adjust frequency/amplitude to see how wave patterns change and interact with barriers.

lessons.title: Refraction and Lenses
objectives: Describe the formation of images by lenses
concepts: Refractive index n = sin i / sin r.

experiment.description: Ray Box: Shine a light ray through a glass block or lens and drag a protractor to measure the angles of incidence and refraction.

lessons.title: Current, Voltage, and Resistance
objectives: Differentiate between series and parallel circuits
concepts: Current (I) is the flow of charge.
Resistance (R) opposes the flow of current.

experiment.description: Circuit Lab: Drag batteries, resistors, and bulbs onto a board to build circuits. Measure current and voltage using a virtual multimeter.

lessons.title: Radioactivity and Half-Life
objectives: Describe alpha, beta, and gamma radiation
concepts: Half-life is the time taken for half the nuclei to decay.

experiment.description: Half-Life Clock: Start with 100 radioactive atoms and watch them 'pop' randomly over time, plotting the remaining atoms on a graph.
